stem: Triangles PQR and STU are congruent, where P corresponds to S, and Q and T are right angles. The measure of angle P is 24°. What is the measure of angle U?
rationale: Choice C is correct. Since the triangles are congruent and P↔S, Q↔T, it follows R↔U. In triangle PQR, angle Q = 90°, so angle R = 180 − 90 − 24 = 66°. Therefore angle U = 66°.
